briefdescription.para: This function is the main entry point for the interrupt example using the XCsi2TxSs driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function will set up the system with interrupts handlers.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique device ID of the MIPI CSI2Tx Subsystem core.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the system setup failed.
XST_SUCCESS should never return since this function, if setup was successful, is blocking.

simplesect.para: If system setup was successful, this function is blocking in order to illustrate interrupt handling.

briefdescription.para: This function initialize required platform-specifc peripherals.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if required peripherals are initialized and configured successfully.

briefdescription.para: This function sets up the interrupt system so interrupts can occur for the MIPI CSI2Tx Subsystem core.

detaileddescription.para: The function is application-specific since the actual system may or may not have an interrupt controller. The MIPI CSI Subsystem core could be directly connected to a processor without an interrupt controller. The user should modify this function to fit the application.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to interrupt controller

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if interrupt setup was successful.
A specific error code defined in "xstatus.h" if an error occurs.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when Wrong number of lanes are configured by the MIPI CSI2Tx Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi2TxSs instance.
of interrupt which caused this event

simplesect.para: Use the XCsi2TxSs_SetCallback driver function to set this function as the handler for wrong lane configuration event.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when Generic Short Packet FIFO is found full by the MIPI CSI2Tx Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi2TxSs instance.
of interrupt which caused this event

simplesect.para: Use the XCsi2TxSs_SetCallback driver function to set this function as the handler for Packet level error event.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when there is a change in ULPS state of D-PHY Lanes.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi2TxSs instance.
of interrupt which caused this event

simplesect.para: Use the XCsi2TxSs_SetCallback driver function to set this function as the handler for ULPS mode detection event.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when a Line buffer is full event received by MIPI CSI2Tx Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi2TxSs instance.
of interrupt which caused this event

simplesect.para: Use the XCsi2TxSs_SetCallback driver function to set this function as the handler for Line buffer full event.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when a event generated by unsupported data type in GSP request to the MIPI CSI2Tx Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi2TxSs instance.
of interrupt which caused this event

simplesect.para: Use the XCsi2TxSs_SetCallback driver function to set this function as the handler for Short Packet FIFO error event.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when during packet transmission, byte stream FIFO is found to be starves for pixel by the MIPI CSI2Tx Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi2TxSs instance.
of interrupt which caused this event

simplesect.para: Use the XCsi2TxSs_SetCallback driver function to set this function as the handler for Byte stream FIFO starve event.

briefdescription.para: For Microblaze we use an assembly loop that is roughly the same regardless of optimization level, although caches and memory access time can make the delay vary.

detaileddescription.para: Just keep in mind that after resetting or updating the PHY modes, the PHY typically needs time to recover.

briefdescription.para: This is the main function for XCsi2TxSs interrupt example.

detaileddescription.para: This is the main function for XCsi2TxSs self test example.

If the Csi2TxSs_IntrExample function which sets up the system succeeds, this function will wait for the interrupts. Once a connection event or pulse is detected, Csi2TxSs will TX device capabilities and re-start the subsystem.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the interrupt example was unsuccessful.
XST_SUCCESS if the interrupt example was successful.
XST_SUCCESS if the self test example passed.
XST_FAILURE if the self test example was unsuccessful.
